name: seq_to_set_rec_empty_is_empty
reason: Empty sequence produces empty set — tests the base case directly

name: seq_to_set_rec_singleton_contains
reason: Singleton sequence's set contains the element — tests one-step recursion

name: seq_to_set_rec_always_finite
reason: The resulting set is always finite — direct consequence of the verified lemma

name: seq_to_set_rec_last_in_set
code:
proof fn phi_4_seq_to_set_rec_last_in_set(seq: Seq<nat>)
    requires
        seq.len() > 0,
    ensures
        seq_to_set_rec(seq).contains(seq.last()),
{
}
reason: The last element is always in the set — follows from the recursive definition inserting `seq.last()`, but only the last element is guaranteed per step

name: seq_to_set_rec_len_leq_seq_len
reason: The set can be strictly smaller than the sequence due to deduplication — the set size is bounded by but not equal to the sequence length, so callers cannot rely on a bijection
